key_insight: Pretrained physics-augmented neural networks can be embedded directly into explicit finite element solvers to model hyperelasticity with physics constraints and practical computational efficiency.
engineering_relevance: This enables machine-learning-based constitutive laws to be used in existing impact and large-strain simulations in Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss without needing a custom solver.
method: Train PANNs in PyTorch with embedded physical constraints
Export network architecture and weights to a standalone Fortran user material routine
Optimize runtime by using faster activations such as SQuarePlus instead of SoftPlus
evidence_signal: An example impact simulation shows the generated user material reproduces the nonlinear large-strain response of hyperelastic materials, and the paper evaluates how network architecture affects simulation performance.
watch_item: Deployment depends on correct network export and efficient evaluation inside explicit solvers, so architecture choice can strongly affect runtime cost.
paper_title: Implementation of Hyperelastic Physics-Augmented Neural Networks in the Explicit Finite Element Codes Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss with Applications to Impact Events
